title: Huberantha luensis
intro: Huberantha luensis, also known as the Lu island tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Huberantha luensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and cytotoxic activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Huberantha luensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
